title: Module Check: Backtracking, Union-Find, Debates, and Gaps

questions.question: In backtracking, why must you `result.append(path[:])` instead of `result.append(path)`?
questions.answer: path is a mutable reference; appending without copying stores a reference that later becomes empty. `path[:]` makes a snapshot copy.

questions.question: What do the three steps of the backtracking template (choose, explore, unchoose) accomplish?
questions.answer: Choose: make a decision and update state. Explore: recurse. Unchoose: undo the decision so siblings start from the same state.

questions.question: What does it mean when DSU `union(u, v)` returns False?
questions.answer: u and v are already in the same connected component — adding this edge would create a cycle

questions.question: Name the two optimisations that give Union-Find O(α(n)) amortised complexity.
questions.answer: Path compression (during find) and union by rank (during union)

questions.question: What minimum test does greedy require before you can trust it over DP?
questions.answer: Try 2-3 counterexamples; if none exist, attempt an exchange argument showing swapping doesn't improve the result
